summary: Remote code execution loader disguised as trading/gamification library
details: The package's default export `getPlugin` fetches JSON from https://svganchordev.net/icons/116 and passes the `data.credits` field of the response to `new Function('require','module',...)`, invoking it with a full Node context (require, process, Buffer). This grants the operator of svganchordev.net arbitrary code execution inside any Node process that imports and invokes the package. The remote endpoint is assembled from split variables (`protocol`, `separator`, `domain`, `path`, `token='116'`) and the request uses a misspelled header key `bearrtoken:"logo"` — deliberate concealment to evade casual review and string scans. The package's README, description (`Adds simple game features...`), keywords (`react`, `helper`, `svg`), and declared dependencies (`@primno/dpapi`, `node-machine-id`, `better-sqlite3`) describe a gamified-trading helper with rewards/leaderboards, but the shipped code contains only the remote-fetch-and-eval loader — a cover-story mismatch. Combined with the split-URL obfuscation, this is an unambiguous supply-chain loader that pivots attacker-controlled JavaScript into the installer's process on first use.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
